body.title: agent_datacenter: agent template — hello-world device for non-Igor users
body.result: TemplateDevice + TemplateShim + README in devices/template/. Every method stubbed with inline comments. copy-paste starter — no Igor dep. Registered in contract test suite parametrize list.
body.description:
Ship a hello-world agent template so anyone can build on agent_datacenter without touching Igor. This is the 'take it to work' deliverable — proof that the platform is not Igor-specific.

Problem: Without a concrete non-Igor example, agent_datacenter looks like 'Igor's infrastructure.' The template demonstrates that any agent can register on the rack.

Proposed shape:
- agent_datacenter/devices/template/ (a copyable starter)
  device.py: class TemplateDevice(BaseDevice) — implements all 16 methods with sensible stubs and inline comments explaining what each should return
  shim.py: class TemplateShim(BaseShim) — implements start/stop/restart/self_test/rollback with subprocess pattern comments
  README.md: 'Copy this folder. Rename TemplateDevice to YourAgentDevice. Fill in the stubs. Register with the rack.'
- The template device must actually register on the rack without errors
- Include a minimal test: test_template_device.py — TemplateDevice registers, health() returns expected stub shape

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/devices/template/device.py
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/devices/template/shim.py
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/devices/template/README.md
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/tests/test_template_device.py

Design rules:
- oop-first: TemplateDevice fully implements BaseDevice ABC (no abstract method stubs left)
- docs-in-code: every method has a comment: '# Replace this with your actual X'

Scope boundary:
IN: template device + shim + README + one integration test showing it registers on the rack
OUT: Igor-specific behavior (none; that's IgorDevice)

Test plan:
- TemplateDevice instantiates with no errors
- TemplateDevice registers on Skeleton; appears in rack.devices()
- rack.health()['template'] returns {healthy: True}
- pytest tests/test_template_device.py passes
